texts.orig: Development of  gas  separation membranes in Japanese  'C,  Chemistry'  Project
texts.text: Development of  gas  separation membranes in Japanese  'C,  Chemistry'  Project

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: K. Haraya, T. Hakuta, K. Obata,  Y. Shindo, N. Itoh, K. Wakabayashi and H. Yoshitome

texts.label: text

texts.text: National  Chemical Laboratory  for Industry, Yatabe, Tsukuba, lbaraki 305,  Japan

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The performances  of  membranes and their modules developed in  Japanese 'Ct  Chemistry'  Project  are summarized. The  new  membranes which  are  an asymmetric polyimide, an asymmetric poly(sulphoneamide), a  plasma  polymer  and a porous  glass membrane have high selectivities enough to  separate and adjust the compositions of the synthesis  gas. Some of the membrane modules have  higher flux  and selectivity than those  of modules commercialized at present.
texts.text: The performances  of  membranes and their modules developed in  Japanese 'Ct  Chemistry'  Project  are summarized. The  new  membranes which  are  an asymmetric polyimide, an asymmetric poly(sulphoneamide), a  plasma  polymer  and a porous  glass membrane have high selectivities enough to  separate and adjust the compositions of the synthesis  gas. Some of the membrane modules have  higher flux  and selectivity than those  of modules commercialized at present.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Separation systems with membrane processes are  designed to  produce the  raw gases of fundamental organic  substances from  a coal gasification  gas. Three  systems,  which employ porous glass membrane modules only,  polyimide  membrane modules only and  both membrane modules,  can provide Hz-CO mixtures  with appropriate concentrations. The latter two systems are  more  economical than the competitive  process.
texts.text: Separation systems with membrane processes are  designed to  produce the  raw gases of fundamental organic  substances from  a coal gasification  gas. Three  systems,  which employ porous glass membrane modules only,  polyimide  membrane modules only and  both membrane modules,  can provide Hz-CO mixtures  with appropriate concentrations. The latter two systems are  more  economical than the competitive  process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Keywords: hydrogen separation membrane; polyimide; poly(sulphone-amide); plasma polymer; porous glass
texts.text: Keywords: hydrogen separation membrane; polyimide; poly(sulphone-amide); plasma polymer; porous glass

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Since Japan  has no  resource  of petroleum,  Japanese chemical industries are  planning to  exchange currently used naphtha  for synthesis gas as a raw material  for organic  chemicals. MIT1 of  Japanese  Government  started a  project called 'C, Chemistry' to support  the  research and development  of new  catalysts for reactions  and  new membranes for  separation  and adjusting feed  compositions. It was started from the  fiscal year  of  1980  as a  7  year project and was finished in  1986. The aims of  this paper are  to  summarize  the  performances of  newly developed membranes and to discuss  the  possibility for  applying membrane  processes to  'C, Chemistry'  reaction processes.
texts.text: Since Japan  has no  resource  of petroleum,  Japanese chemical industries are  planning to  exchange currently used naphtha  for synthesis gas as a raw material  for organic  chemicals. MIT1 of  Japanese  Government  started a  project called 'C, Chemistry' to support  the  research and development  of new  catalysts for reactions  and  new membranes for  separation  and adjusting feed  compositions. It was started from the  fiscal year  of  1980  as a  7  year project and was finished in  1986. The aims of  this paper are  to  summarize  the  performances of  newly developed membranes and to discuss  the  possibility for  applying membrane  processes to  'C, Chemistry'  reaction processes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  membrane process  for  gas separation  has been successfully commercialized  by  several  companies1-4 using  polymer  membranes, namely,  a  composite  membrane of silicone resin  and  microporous  polysulphone, and asymmetric cellulose acetate membranes. For  the  purpose of  widely  spreading the  membrane process, it is important to  develop  new membranes  which have resistance to  high temperature and  to  various  chemical substances. This project plans  to  develop  nonporous and porous  membranes from  glassy polymers  with  a  high  glass transition temperature  (Ts), polymeric materials formed by plasma polymerization  and an inorganic  material of  silica glass in  co-operation  with  three  Japanese companie?.  These materials are  attractive for  applying  to  membranes,  but preparing  membranes for  commercial  use  has not  been successful. Major breakthrough points  are  as  follows:
texts.text: The  membrane process  for  gas separation  has been successfully commercialized  by  several  companies1-4 using  polymer  membranes, namely,  a  composite  membrane of silicone resin  and  microporous  polysulphone, and asymmetric cellulose acetate membranes. For  the  purpose of  widely  spreading the  membrane process, it is important to  develop  new membranes  which have resistance to  high temperature and  to  various  chemical substances. This project plans  to  develop  nonporous and porous  membranes from  glassy polymers  with  a  high  glass transition temperature  (Ts), polymeric materials formed by plasma polymerization  and an inorganic  material of  silica glass in  co-operation  with  three  Japanese companie?.  These materials are  attractive for  applying  to  membranes,  but preparing  membranes for  commercial  use  has not  been successful. Major breakthrough points  are  as  follows:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1 nonporous polymer membranes generally have anisotropic structure consisting of  a  surface skin  layer on a  porous  sublayer in  order to  have  reasonable  flux.
texts.text: 1 nonporous polymer membranes generally have anisotropic structure consisting of  a  surface skin  layer on a  porous  sublayer in  order to  have  reasonable  flux.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This type  of  membrane, namely asymmetric membranes, can  be  prepared  using a  'phase inversion' technique  originally developed by Loeb and Souriraja#. However, nominally  dense  skin  layers of glassy  polymer  asymmetric membranes sometimes exhibit  surface  porosity  resulting in decrease of selectivity. Itis  difficult to  prepare  the  flawless skin layers for  glassy asymmetric membranes;

For the  porous glass membrane,  development of  an adhesive  material which is  necessary  to fabricate membrane modules is  a  major subject.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Sumitomo Electric Industries, Ltd.  has  developed a composite membrane consisting of three layers of an ultrathin plasma polymer,  an  intermediate  layer and  a porous PTFE substrate. Polyphenylene oxide is coated on the  porous PTFE to  seal pores and plays  a  role  of  primer to improve  adhesion  strength  between  the plasma  polymer and  the PTFE substrate.  Plasma polymers were  polymerized  from various organic monomers of  over 50  kinds

Among the  monomers tested, silane  compounds containing unsaturated  bond  like  a vinyl group  or an ethenyl group yield  a  high selectivity for  a  Hz-CO mixture (permeability ratio  of Hz/CO > 300)  whereas, unfortunately the permeability  is substantially smaller  than  that of the asymmetric polyimide. texts.label: text
texts.orig: Toyobo Co., Ltd. has  developed  poly(sulphone-amide) copolymers synthesized  from  isophthaloyl chloride and mixed diamines consisting  of bis(4-(4-aminophenox ) phenyl) sulphone and m-phenylenediamine (MPD) 6 . This polymer  has a  high T,  of  - 520 K and exhibits  a  high selectivity  for premeation of  a  HZ-CO mixture.  The selectivity increases with increasing the  MPD content in mixed  diamines,  because  a high concentration  of  amide linkage on  the polymer chains  restricts the diffusion of CO.  Hollow fibre membranes were  prepared  from  the polymer  N,N-dimethylacetamide solution using a dry-jet and  wet spinning  method followed by coagulation  in a water bath and  drying by a solvent-exchange technique. Gas  permeation rates  through  a hollow  fibre  membrane are shown in Figure 2. At 373 K, the selectivity for a Hz-CO system is  slightly higher than that  of  the  polyimide shown in Figure 1, while  permeation rate  is  somewhat smaller.  As can be seen fromFi&z@el?,  Arrhenius  type  plots of  permeability vs. temperature exhibit  curved lines, which suggest a  contamination of the pore  flow12g 13. Although the selectivity is  reasonable  for  practical  use, there  is  a still room for  improving selectivity.
texts.text: Toyobo Co., Ltd. has  developed  poly(sulphone-amide) copolymers synthesized  from  isophthaloyl chloride and mixed diamines consisting  of bis(4-(4-aminophenox ) phenyl) sulphone and m-phenylenediamine (MPD) 6 . This polymer  has a  high T,  of  - 520 K and exhibits  a  high selectivity  for premeation of  a  HZ-CO mixture.  The selectivity increases with increasing the  MPD content in mixed  diamines,  because  a high concentration  of  amide linkage on  the polymer chains  restricts the diffusion of CO.  Hollow fibre membranes were  prepared  from  the polymer  N,N-dimethylacetamide solution using a dry-jet and  wet spinning  method followed by coagulation  in a water bath and  drying by a solvent-exchange technique. Gas  permeation rates  through  a hollow  fibre  membrane are shown in Figure 2. At 373 K, the selectivity for a Hz-CO system is  slightly higher than that  of  the  polyimide shown in Figure 1, while  permeation rate  is  somewhat smaller.  As can be seen fromFi&z@el?,  Arrhenius  type  plots of  permeability vs. temperature exhibit  curved lines, which suggest a  contamination of the pore  flow12g 13. Although the selectivity is  reasonable  for  practical  use, there  is  a still room for  improving selectivity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Toyobo has  developed porous glass membranes also. Capillary membranes were  spun  from  the melted  borosilicate  glass followed  by  acid  treatment  resulting in microporous structure due  to leaching  sodium borate. Membranes which have  porosity of 0.5 and  controlled pore  diameters  of 3 - 30 nm were  prepared  and their permeation  behaviours  were measured14. The  permeation of  mixed  gas  follows  essentially Knudsen flow  mechanism, but becomes decreasing selectivity as  a  result of  collisions of  different  gas molecules  (Present-deBethune's  effect') with increasing the  pore size. In addition,  concentration polarization  on the  membrane surface  appears remarkably above 20 nm of pore  size.  Membrane modules were constructed  using  membranes which have  11  nm pore diameter. They  were  bundled by  an  adhesion material, which  is  a  compound of  glass  and  ceramics  and  have the same  ratio  of  thermal expansion as  that  of  the  membrane.
texts.text: Toyobo has  developed porous glass membranes also. Capillary membranes were  spun  from  the melted  borosilicate  glass followed  by  acid  treatment  resulting in microporous structure due  to leaching  sodium borate. Membranes which have  porosity of 0.5 and  controlled pore  diameters  of 3 - 30 nm were  prepared  and their permeation  behaviours  were measured14. The  permeation of  mixed  gas  follows  essentially Knudsen flow  mechanism, but becomes decreasing selectivity as  a  result of  collisions of  different  gas molecules  (Present-deBethune's  effect') with increasing the  pore size. In addition,  concentration polarization  on the  membrane surface  appears remarkably above 20 nm of pore  size.  Membrane modules were constructed  using  membranes which have  11  nm pore diameter. They  were  bundled by  an  adhesion material, which  is  a  compound of  glass  and  ceramics  and  have the same  ratio  of  thermal expansion as  that  of  the  membrane.

texts.orig: All membrane modules are  constructed similar to  the shell-and-tube type  heat  exchanger, but there  is a difference in  the feed point  of gases. Modules of the polyimide and the  plasma polymer membrane were designed  to make the gas flow of feed and  permeate  a countercurrent  flow pattern.  Feed  gas is  supplied  from one end of  the  outer  shell. Modules of  the  poly(sulphoneamide)  and  the  porous  glass  membrane were designed  to

texts.orig: xf,  x, and  y are mole  fractions  of H2  on  feed, nonpermeate and  permeate streams,  respectively make the  gas  flow  a  cross flow  pattern, by  feeding gas  from a distribution tube inserted  in the centre  of the libre bundle.  Performances of  modules are  strongly affected by flow  patterns in  the  range  of  large stage cut  (flow  ratio of permeate  to feed)16. Exact analyses of modules  need complicated mathematical  models. In this  paper, for simplicity, we assumed a  perfect mixing model to  express permeability and selectivity. In  separation tests of  HI-CO binary mixtures,  HI  content of nonpermeate, x,,  and permeate,y, and flow  rate of  permeate, Qp,  were  measured. Replacing  mole fractions of Hz with these  of CO  in  Equation (1) yields  a nominal permeability of  CO. In  the  calculation, data  measured at small  stage cuts  were used  to  avoid  large  deviation from the  true  permeability of  membranes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Table I shows the some  data of miniature  modules whose permeation capacities  are  around 100mm3 (STP) s-' at Ap  = 5  MPa. Module performances  are reflected directly by  membrane's  performances. These results show the  modules  of  the  polymeric membranes can  be  used  at T  = 373  K and Ap  = 5  MPa  and that of  the porous  glass membrane can  be  used  at T = 673  K,  at  least.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In succession, pilot-scale modules  were constructed and tested using  synthesis gases  (syngas) produced by a coal gasification process of Texaco  method.  A  flow diagram of  the  pilot plant  is illustrated in Figure  3. Syngas is  initially cooled  and water  vapour removed  by a  drain separator. Sour gases  (H$,  CO2, COS, etc.) are  absorbed in cold methanol. After  purification feed gas contains 45  + 3% HZ, 52  + 3% CO,  3% N2  and trace  of C02, H2S and methanol,  and then  it is  compressed and heated  for supplying  to  four  modules located in  parallel. Over three months,  test runs  were  carried out  at various temperatures, pressure  differences and stage cuts. DetaiIs of  the  results are omitted here,  but it  should be  emphasized  that modules of  the  polyimide  and the  poly(sulphone-amide)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To examine the  durability, continuous  operation  was carried out  for  1000  h,  using  another  module.  The results of  continuous  runs  are  shown  in Figures  4,  5,  6 and 7  in which permeation rates are  again  nominal.  All  modules' performances are  generally stable, although  a  decrease  of Hz permeability is  observed  in  the  module  of  polyimide (Ml) for  an initial period of 500  h.  Scatters of data  in module  Ml  seem to  arise from the  instability of  the  feed flow rate, because module  Ml  was located  nearest  the compressor and was affected directly by instability of  the supply  from the  gasification process. The selectivity of  the module  of the plasma polymer  membrane is  reduced drastically. It  seems that  a channelling  or  by-pass  flow occurs  in  the  large diameter  module. It was necessary  to construct a large shell because  of  its small  permeability. It is expected  that selectivity will recover  by redesigning the module into the  cross flow  pattern, with  a  core  of  feed  gas distributor.
texts.text: To examine the  durability, continuous  operation  was carried out  for  1000  h,  using  another  module.  The results of  continuous  runs  are  shown  in Figures  4,  5,  6 and 7  in which permeation rates are  again  nominal.  All  modules' performances are  generally stable, although  a  decrease  of Hz permeability is  observed  in  the  module  of  polyimide (Ml) for  an initial period of 500  h.  Scatters of data  in module  Ml  seem to  arise from the  instability of  the  feed flow rate, because module  Ml  was located  nearest  the compressor and was affected directly by instability of  the supply  from the  gasification process. The selectivity of  the module  of the plasma polymer  membrane is  reduced drastically. It  seems that  a channelling  or  by-pass  flow occurs  in  the  large diameter  module. It was necessary  to construct a large shell because  of  its small  permeability. It is expected  that selectivity will recover  by redesigning the module into the  cross flow  pattern, with  a  core  of  feed  gas distributor.

texts.orig: Design of synthesis gas  separation process
texts.text: Design of synthesis gas  separation process

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In  principle, high-degree  separation is  possible usin  a cascade system or continuous membrane 7g column' -19, even  if membrane selectivity is small. However, in  order  to make a  membrane separation process economical compared  to  other  competitive  processes, an optimum system must be designed for  a specific process. In the following, membrane separation processes  are  designed for  the  purpose  of  producing syngases with  various  mole ratios which are  suitable to  reaction  stoichiometries of basic organic substances such as methanol, ethylene glycol, acetic acid  and so  on.
texts.text: In  principle, high-degree  separation is  possible usin  a cascade system or continuous membrane 7g column' -19, even  if membrane selectivity is small. However, in  order  to make a  membrane separation process economical compared  to  other  competitive  processes, an optimum system must be designed for  a specific process. In the following, membrane separation processes  are  designed for  the  purpose  of  producing syngases with  various  mole ratios which are  suitable to  reaction  stoichiometries of basic organic substances such as methanol, ethylene glycol, acetic acid  and so  on.

Selectivities of  both  membranes  are  set less than  their potential  values,  because the separation  with the porous glass  membrane is  lowered by the  PresentdeBethune's effect  and  that  with the asymmetric polyimide  hollow  fibre is lowered  by  the  pressure loss in  the  fibre bore;  and

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The serial fed multistage  system can  separate  large amounts  of feed  gas  without  difficulty in  its operation, while  the  parallel fed  multistage system needs multiple flow rate  regulators to  provide  uniform  feed  flow.  The continuous membrane column  can attain  high-degree separation with  porous  glass membrane of  low-selectivity.
texts.text: The serial fed multistage  system can  separate  large amounts  of feed  gas  without  difficulty in  its operation, while  the  parallel fed  multistage system needs multiple flow rate  regulators to  provide  uniform  feed  flow.  The continuous membrane column  can attain  high-degree separation with  porous  glass membrane of  low-selectivity.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Three  systems are studied, i.e., Case  1 is  a system consisting of  modules of  porous  glass membranes;  Case 2 is  a system consisting of  both modules  of  porous glass membranes and polyimide membranes;  and Case 3  is  a system  consisting of  modules of  polyimide membranes.
texts.text: Three  systems are studied, i.e., Case  1 is  a system consisting of  modules of  porous  glass membranes;  Case 2 is  a system consisting of  both modules  of  porous glass membranes and polyimide membranes;  and Case 3  is  a system  consisting of  modules of  polyimide membranes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Calculated results for three systems  are  shown inF&res  8, 9  and  IO. In  Case 1, the  gas  of  60% H2-40%  CO  is obtained obtained  in  the  permeate of  the  first section, and gases  of 66% Hz-34%  CO  and 3% Hz-97%  CO  are  obtained  respectively in  the  permeate and nonpermeate  of  the  second section  which  is the continuous  membrane column composed of 20 modules. In Case 2,  the first section employs  porous glass  membranes and produces gas  of 60% H&Y% CO. The  second section which is  the  serial fed multistage  system composed of 9 modules  of the polyimide membrane produces pure CO in the nonpermeate and 66% Hz-34%  CO  in  the  permeate.  In  Case  3, the gas 66% Hz-34%  CO obtained in the permeate  of the  second section which is the  serial fed  multistage of  9 modules.  Pure CO  is obtained  in  the  nonpermeate  of  the second  section, and the  gas of  60% H+O% CO  is produced by blending the permeate of the first section  and  the nonpermeate  of  the  second section.
texts.text: Calculated results for three systems  are  shown inF&res  8, 9  and  IO. In  Case 1, the  gas  of  60% H2-40%  CO  is obtained obtained  in  the  permeate of  the  first section, and gases  of 66% Hz-34%  CO  and 3% Hz-97%  CO  are  obtained  respectively in  the  permeate and nonpermeate  of  the  second section  which  is the continuous  membrane column composed of 20 modules. In Case 2,  the first section employs  porous glass  membranes and produces gas  of 60% H&Y% CO. The  second section which is  the  serial fed multistage  system composed of 9 modules  of the polyimide membrane produces pure CO in the nonpermeate and 66% Hz-34%  CO  in  the  permeate.  In  Case  3, the gas 66% Hz-34%  CO obtained in the permeate  of the  second section which is the  serial fed  multistage of  9 modules.  Pure CO  is obtained  in  the  nonpermeate  of  the second  section, and the  gas of  60% H+O% CO  is produced by blending the permeate of the first section  and  the nonpermeate  of  the  second section.

texts.text: with other processes.  For  instance, an  operating cost  of Cosorb  process,  which is  an  absorption  process of CO, was  estimated  as 24 per  1  m3  (STP)  of the feed under similar conditions22.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The performances of  membranes and their modules developed  in Japanese  'Ct  Chemistry'  Project have  been summarized. The  membranes have high enough selectivities to  separate  and adjust  the  compositions of  the synthesis gas. Some of the  membrane modules have higher  flux  and selectivity than those  of  present  commercial modules.
texts.text: The performances of  membranes and their modules developed  in Japanese  'Ct  Chemistry'  Project have  been summarized. The  membranes have high enough selectivities to  separate  and adjust  the  compositions of  the synthesis gas. Some of the  membrane modules have higher  flux  and selectivity than those  of  present  commercial modules.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Separation systems  with  membrane processes  were designed to  produce  the  raw gases  of  fundamental organic substances  from  a coal gasification  gas.  Three  systems, which employ porous  glass membrane modules only, polyimide  membrane modules only and  both membrane modules,  can  provide  H2-CO mixtures  with appropriate concentrations.  The latter two systems are  more  economical than  the competitive process.
texts.text: Separation systems  with  membrane processes  were designed to  produce  the  raw gases  of  fundamental organic substances  from  a coal gasification  gas.  Three  systems, which employ porous  glass membrane modules only, polyimide  membrane modules only and  both membrane modules,  can  provide  H2-CO mixtures  with appropriate concentrations.  The latter two systems are  more  economical than  the competitive process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This work  is  a  part  of  the  results of  'Cl  Chemistry Project, a National  Research  and  Development Program of the Agency  of  Industrial  Science and Technology,  Ministry of International Trade  and  Industry (M.I.T.I.), Japan.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Cost were estimated  based on Japanese yen and were expressed  by US $  with  a  exchange ratio of  1  $  = 140  yen. Porous  glass membrane  was  assumed to be  50  000  yen mm2 of  membrane area, and its life was assumed to  be  5  years. Polyimide membrane was assumed to  be 30  000  yen mm2 of  membrane area, and its life was assumed to  be  3  years. Power  consumption  was calculated assuming that  two stage compression  was employed with  an  efficiency of  0.8. The cost of  electric power was  assumed to be  10  yen  kWh-'. 8000  hours  was assumed as  operating  time  of  one year.
texts.text: Cost were estimated  based on Japanese yen and were expressed  by US $  with  a  exchange ratio of  1  $  = 140  yen. Porous  glass membrane  was  assumed to be  50  000  yen mm2 of  membrane area, and its life was assumed to  be  5  years. Polyimide membrane was assumed to  be 30  000  yen mm2 of  membrane area, and its life was assumed to  be  3  years. Power  consumption  was calculated assuming that  two stage compression  was employed with  an  efficiency of  0.8. The cost of  electric power was  assumed to be  10  yen  kWh-'. 8000  hours  was assumed as  operating  time  of  one year.
